Summary:

The paper discusses the analysis of malicious and benign data using various visualization techniques. The authors present three figures: Table 4, Figure 5, and Figure 6. Table 4 provides a visual representation of the distribution of malicious and benign data, while Figure 5 and Figure 6 showcase the distribution of malicious data across individual variables and highlight outliers. The authors also discuss the importance of feature selection using the MDI method and XGBoost.

Categorization:

I would categorize this paper as "Demonstrated". The paper provides a comprehensive analysis of malicious and benign data, highlighting the importance of visualization techniques and feature selection. The authors demonstrate the effectiveness of their approach by presenting several figures and providing insights into the dataset. The paper does not present hypothetical scenarios or describe active exploitation techniques, but rather focuses on analyzing and understanding the characteristics of the dataset.
